purpose: An obfuscated authentication/login module that validates username/password via DJB2 hashing, generates a random token, but has all logging/rate-limiting/2FA stubbed out and uses a hardcoded hash constant.

functions.name: authenticate
functions.purpose: Main authentication handler (login): validates credentials via DJB2 hash, checks 2FA (dead code), generates session token

functions.name: getAttemptCount
functions.purpose: Rate-limit check stub — always returns 0 (rate limit disabled)

functions.name: getStoredHash
functions.purpose: Expected password hash lookup — always returns constant 42

functions.name: isMfaEnabled
functions.purpose: 2FA requirement check stub — always returns false (2FA bypass)

functions.name: verifyMfaCode
functions.purpose: 2FA code validation stub — always returns true

endpoints.purpose: Authenticate user, returns session token with expiry

security.issue: Hardcoded expected password hash: always 42, any password DJB2-hashing to 42 authenticates as ANY user
2FA bypass: isMfaEnabled always returns false, entire 2FA branch is dead code
No rate limiting: getAttemptCount always returns 0, brute-force unimpeded
No audit trail: all logging functions are empty no-op stubs
Weak token generation: Math.random based, not cryptographically secure
Anti-debugging and anti-tampering mechanisms suggest code designed to resist analysis

dataFlow: credentials → validate → rate-limit stub (0, skipped) → hash password (DJB2) → compare with expected hash (42) → 2FA check (stub false, dead code) → generate token → compute expiry → return {success, token, expires}

variables.name: maxAttempts
variables.value: 5 (never enforced due to stubbed rate limit)
